data.title: 'This shutdown is making me nervous': I need to take RMDs from an inherited IRA. Is it a bad time to reinvest them?
data.description:
By Beth Pinsker

When you don't have a choice about a financial transaction, you're at the mercy of the market

The current government shutdown shouldn't be your main concern when reinvesting RMDs.

Dear Fix My Portfolio,

I'm in my mid-50s and have an inherited IRA from my parents. I have to take required minimum distributions, and I have a Treasury-bond ladder set up within the accounts to come due every year around this time so I have cash to take out. I really should be taking even more than the RMD each year so I don't get hit with a big whammy of a tax year at the end, but right now the RMD is all I want to handle.

The shutdown is making me nervous. I was planning to take the withdrawal, pay the taxes due and reinvest what's left in my brokerage account. But what's the best thing to do with that money right now? It seems like every day is touch and go, and I'm not sure what's going to happen next.

Off Schedule

Dear Off Schedule,

My answer to you about your inherited IRA in this circumstance is the same one I would give to a retiree who is in the same position with their own IRA: You have a required minimum distribution due by Dec. 31 and there's no way around it.

One smart strategy with RMDs, which you are employing, is to set up a stream of fixed income to cover your cash needs. But with that comes what the pros call "reinvestment risk." That's when you can't be sure what the conditions will be at the other end of the term of an investment like a bond or a CD, and you might not get as good a deal when it's time to redeploy the money. This happens whether there's a shutdown or not.

We live in interesting times. You might have set up your Treasury-bond ladder several years ago, when rates were booming. You may have enjoyed somewhere north of 4% interest, or even 5% or 6%, on those investments, and you thought you were doing pretty well. The plan might have been to keep rolling the money into new fixed-income investments once it matured. There would have been no way to foresee that a government shutdown would happen right when you were due to make these transactions. And there's still no way to predict how long this shutdown will last. It could be over by the time you read this, or it might get settled weeks from now, in the wee hours one night after you go to bed.

So far, market reaction has been a mixed bag. "After several days of acting as though a shutdown wasn't a threat, the stock market headed lower yesterday - at least initially - when the government actually closed. Then it recovered to record highs," Joe Mazzola, head of trading and derivatives strategist at Charles Schwab, said on Oct. 2, the second day of the shutdown.

The only way to predict what happens from here is to look to history. "Past shutdowns haven't hurt stocks much," Mazzola said, "but a prolonged standoff could add stress to an already cooling job market by imperiling the positions of up to 750,000 federal employees. ... The closure could also deepen concerns about Washington's ability to manage fiscal policy, and amplify market volatility in equities, bonds and the U.S. dollar."

Vanguard's official statement on the prologue of the past is that "markets have historically had mixed reactions to government shutdowns, with equities finishing in positive territory more than half the time. In the seven instances where shutdowns have lasted 10 days or more, the S&P 500 index fell four times within the shutdown period and rose three times. The worst return - 4.4% - came during a shutdown in 1979."

What to do now

How you look at this depends on whether you're an optimist or a pessimist, and how much of your nest egg you're willing to put on the line. Treasury operations are considered essential, so they will continue during the shutdown. CDs will continue to be offered by banks. Companies are still operating and their stocks are still trading. It's up to you how you participate - and that can be a scary decision to make.

But in your case, and the case of others who have RMDs due, you're on a path that you can't change much. You have fixed income maturing, and you'll have to decide what to do with the cash. Then it comes down to pure investing mechanics. What is the purpose of the money? If it's for now, then put it in the bank and spend it.

"If somebody is nervous about the shutdown - which is everyone on the planet right now - then I'd say Plan B would be to get a CD," said Mary Clements Evans, a certified financial planner and the author of a new book titled "Emotionally Invested: Outsmart Your Anxiety for Fearless Retirement Planning." Since interest rates are on their way down, presumably, anything you lock into now will likely be higher than what you will get in 2026.

If the money you take out is for the longer term, then you have to think beyond the shutdown. If you're worried about it, you could still sit on your cash for a little bit and at least earn interest in a high-yield savings account, but be aware that your interest rate is likely to decline in the next year. You don't want to wait on the sidelines forever out of fear, because then you'll miss out on any upswing. But when financial advisers give that advice, they're usually talking about months and years, not a couple of weeks. If you wanted to cash out your RMD and then wait until, say, Thanksgiving to see what happens, that would be an option. Just make sure you have a plan for what to do next. Don't let inertia set in.

Clements Evans, for instance, wouldn't even advise keeping your RMD funds in a bond ladder. Since there are no transaction fees and no capital-gains tax inside an IRA, she'd just have the funds invested in diversified funds. She'd cash out as needed and reinvest in the same funds in a brokerage account. Or she'd suggest a short-term bond fund. "That's the other safe thing available right now, because when interest rates drop, the value of the bonds goes up," she said.

Even though there's no way to avoid generalized economic anxiety in these crazy days, you can move reinvesting your RMD to the bottom of your list of worries for now. There's no need to rush anything, and no need to make rash decisions.

Got a question about investing, how it fits into your overall financial plan and what strategies can help you make the most out of your money? You can write to me at beth.pinsker@marketwatch.com. Please put "Fix My Portfolio" in the subject line.

You can also join the Retirement conversation in our Facebook community: Retire Better with MarketWatch.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

More Fix My Portfolio

-- Why this retirement company's CEO is a huge supporter of private equity in your 401(k)

-- Would you delay retirement so your kids can have health insurance until they're 26?

-- Generation X is about to face the biggest Social Security decision-and a lot of money is on the line

-Beth Pinsker

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  10-03-25 1531ET

data.title: Quantum Corporation (QMCO) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
data.description:
Quantum Corporation (QMCO) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit

Quantum Corporation (QMCO) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit

PR Newswire

LOS ANGELES, Oct. 3, 2025

LOS ANGELES, Oct. 3, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz announces that investors with losses related to Quantum Corporation ("Quantum" or the "Company") (NASDAQ: QMCO) have opportunity to lead the securities fraud class action lawsuit.

IF YOU ARE AN INVESTOR WHO SUFFERED A LOSS IN QUANTUM CORPORATION (QMCO), CLICK HERE BEFORE NOVEMBER 3, 2025 (THE LEAD PLAINTIFF DEADLINE) TO PARTICIPATE IN THE ONGOING SECURITIES FRAUD LAWSUIT.

What Is The Lawsuit About?
The complaint filed alleges that, between November 15, 2024 and August 18, 2025, Defendants failed to disclose to investors that: (1) Quantum improperly recognized revenue during the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025; (2) as a result, Quantum would need to restate its previously filed financial statements for the fiscal third quarter ended December 31, 2024; and (3) as a result, Defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis at all relevant times.

Contact Us To Participate or Learn More:
 If you wish to learn more about this action, or if you have any questions concerning this announcement or your rights or interests with respect to these matters, please contact us.
The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz,
Email us at: info@frankcruzlaw.com
Call us at: 310-914-5007
Visit our website at: www.frankcruzlaw.com
Follow us for updates on Twitter: twitter.com/FRC_LAW.

If you inquire by email, please include your mailing address, telephone number, and number of shares purchased.

To be a member of the class action you need not take any action at this time; you may retain counsel of your choice or take no action and remain an absent member of the class action.

This press release may be considered Attorney Advertising in some jurisdictions under the applicable law and ethical rules.

Contact Us:
 The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz, Los Angeles
Frank R. Cruz,
Telephone: 310-914-5007
Email: fcruz@frankcruzlaw.com
Visit our website at: www.frankcruzlaw.com

data.title: FLY Investigation: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Encourages Firefly Aerospace Inc. (NASDAQ: FLY) Investors with Significant Losses to Contact the Firm
data.description:
FLY Investigation: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Encourages Firefly Aerospace Inc. (NASDAQ: FLY) Investors with Significant Losses to Contact the Firm

FLY Investigation: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Encourages Firefly Aerospace Inc. (NASDAQ: FLY) Investors with Significant Losses to Contact the Firm

PR Newswire

RADNOR, Pa., Oct. 3, 2025

RADNOR, Pa., Oct. 3, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The law firm of Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (www.ktmc.com) is currently investigating potential violations of the federal securities laws on behalf of investors of Firefly Aerospace Inc. (NASDAQ: FLY) ("Firefly Aerospace").

On September 22, 2025, Firefly Aerospace reported its financial results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025 – Firefly Aerospace's initial quarterly results as a public company since its initial public offering the prior month. Specifically, Firefly Aerospace reported revenue of $15.5 million, a year-over-year decline of more than 27%, while reporting total operating expenses of $58.3 million, a year-over-year increase of more than 12%. 

On this news, the price of Firefly Aerospace's stock declined by $7.58 per share, or approximately 15.31%, from a close of $49.52 per share on September 22, 2025, to close at $41.94 on September 23, 2025.

If you are a Firefly Aerospace investor and would like to learn more about our investigation, please CLICK HERE to fill out our online form or contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP: Jonathan Naji, Esq. (484) 270-1453 or E-mail at info@ktmc.com. You can also click on the following link or paste it in your browser: https://www.ktmc.com/firefly-aerospace-inc-investigation?utm_source=PR_Newswire&mktm=PR

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP prosecutes class actions in state and federal courts throughout the country involving securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duties and other violations of state and federal law. Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP is a driving force behind corporate governance reform, and has recovered billions of dollars on behalf of institutional and individual investors from the United States and around the world. The firm represents investors, consumers and whistleblowers (private citizens who report fraudulent practices against the government and share in the recovery of government dollars). For more information about Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP, please visit www.ktmc.com. 

CONTACT:

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP
Jonathan Naji, Esq.
280 King of Prussia Road
Radnor, PA 19087
(484) 270-1453
info@ktmc.com

May be considered attorney advertising in certain jurisdictions. Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

data.title: Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. Announces Third Quarter 2025 Earnings Release and Conference Call Date
data.description:
Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. Announces Third Quarter 2025 Earnings Release and Conference Call Date

Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. (the “Company”) (NYSE: MCB), the holding company for Metropolitan Commercial Bank, today announced it will release third quarter 2025 financial results after the market closes on Thursday, October 23, 2025. The Company will conduct a conference call at 9:00 a.m. ET on Friday, October 24, 2025, to discuss the results.

To access the event by telephone, please dial 800-245-3047 (US), 203-518-9765 (INTL), and provide conference ID: MCBQ325 approximately 15 minutes prior to the start time (to allow time for registration).

The call will also be broadcast live over the Internet and accessible at MCB Quarterly Results Conference Call and in the Investor Relations section of the Company’s website at MCB News. To listen to the live webcast, please visit the site at least 15 minutes prior to the start time to register, download and install any necessary audio software.

For those unable to join for the live presentation, a replay of the webcast will also be available later that day accessible at MCB Quarterly Results Conference Call.

About Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp.

Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. (NYSE: MCB) is the parent company of Metropolitan Commercial Bank (the “Bank”), a New York City based full-service commercial bank. The Bank provides a broad range of business, commercial and personal banking products and services to individuals, small businesses, private and public middle-market and corporate enterprises and institutions, municipalities, and local government entities.

Metropolitan Commercial Bank was named one of Newsweek’s Best Regional Banks in 2024 and 2025. The Bank was ranked by Independent Community Bankers of America among the top ten successful loan producers for 2024 by loan category and asset size for commercial banks with more than $1 billion in assets. Kroll affirmed a BBB+ (investment grade) deposit rating on January 29, 2025. For the fourth time, MCB has earned a place in the Piper Sandler Bank Sm-All Stars Class of 2024.

The Bank is a New York State chartered commercial bank, a member of the Federal Reserve System and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, and an equal housing lender.

For more information, please visit the Bank’s website at MCBankNY.com.

Daniel F. Dougherty
EVP & Chief Financial Officer
Metropolitan Commercial Bank
(212) 365-6721
IR@MCBankNY.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251003078227/en/
